sql_migration_plan.title: SGA Microsoft SQL Server Migration Plan
sql_migration_plan.description: Migration of flat-file database (CSV/JSON) to a robust relational database in Microsoft SQL Server (Express or Standard).

tasks.description: Install Microsoft SQL Server Express or Standard on target machine.
Create the new database 'SGA_Database' in SQL Server. Enable Mixed Mode Authentication to allow an isolated application SQL user.
Install `ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server` on the host machine. Run `pip install pyodbc sqlalchemy` in the virtual environment to support fast DataFrame transfers via Pandas.
Create tables for fixed catalogs: `h_statements`, `p_statements`, and `pictograms`. These represent the absolute master dictionary of GHS codes.
Create `products_master` table with columns like `product_id` (Primary Key), `name`, `signal_word`, `un_number`, `cas_number`.

Create junction tables mapping Products to GHS elements: `product_hazards` (FK ProductID -> FK H-Code), `product_precautions` (FK -> FK P-Code), `product_pictograms` (FK -> FK PictogramName), and `product_variants` (mapping father SKUs to son SKUs).
Create `product_lotes` table specifically extracted from `product_classifications.json` to handle batch control (1-to-many relation: ProductID -> Lote Number, Elaboration Date, Inspection Date).
Replace JSON files with SQL Tables: `users` (Authentication), `print_history` (Audit Log), and `order_statuses` (Live state tracker substitute for order_status_db.json).

Create `sql_migrator.py` to parse current CSVs and dump them cleanly into SQL using `pd.to_sql(name, engine, if_exists='append', index=False)`. It must handle data type casting (e.g., ensuring H-codes stay as Strings, not floats).
Convert `product_classifications.json` into SQL rows, effectively absorbing dynamic tara weights and manual overrides into the `products_master`.
Completely overhaul `database_client.py` and `smart_label.py`. Instead of iterating through loaded pandas global state, formulate `SELECT` and `JOIN` statements via SQLAlchemy.
Update `user_manager.py`, `history_manager.py`, and `tara_weight_manager.py` to perform SQL `UPDATE`/`INSERT` commands instead of JSON `json.dump()` file overwrites.
Run identical label generation logic. Compare SQL return output vs old flat-file return output using `pytest` asserting dictionary matches.
Once validated in production, archive `unified_db/` folder to cold storage. Clean up legacy JSON files across the local operational directories.
